metadata.purpose: Same model, two providers: detect backend divergence (quantization, precision, silent degradation).
metadata.hold-fixed: model + invocationPath + posture are identical across arms; only `provider` varies.
metadata.note: taskIds is intentionally omitted so the WHOLE suite runs. This is a comprehensive assessment, not a per-task test. Read the composite + per-language/per-category sub-scores and the per-task divergences, not any single task.
metadata.why-poetic-adapter: poetic-adapter is the only invocation path that records verified resolved-model evidence -- it binds the provider/model identifiers Poetic reports for each request, so you know which provider+model was REQUESTED and acknowledged. It does NOT independently attest weights, precision, or the physical backend, so it cannot by itself prove a provider is serving an unquantized variant; a quantized proxy returning the expected ids would still be accepted. Detect quantization behaviourally, via consistent per-task divergence across repetitions, and confirm backend identity out-of-band. native-cli / poetic-system record no resolved-model evidence at all and are unsuitable for provider-equivalence claims.
metadata.replace-before-running: Set `provider`/`model` to real ids registered in your Poetic install. The values below are placeholders.
